word: G422 - ἀνεπαίσχυντος - anepaíschyntos - an-ep-ah'-ee-skhoon-tos - Not ashamed

sections.paragraph:
- Original: ἀνεπαίσχυντος

- Transliteration: Anepaischuntos

- Phonetic: an-ep-ah'-ee-skhoon-tos

- Definition:

1. having no cause to be ashamed

- Origin: from G1 (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of a compound of G1909 and G153

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Adjective

- Strong's: From G1 (as a negative particle) and a presumed derivative of a compound of G1909 and G153; not ashamed that is (by implication) irreprehensible: - that neededth not to be ashamed.
